name: Customer pays an invoice via SMS link without ever opening the email
description: Older homeowner customer prefers texts over email. The system also sends an SMS with the public-invoice link. She taps the link from her Messages app; the page renders mobile-first; she pays via Apple Pay; no email account ever touched.

persona.name: Lorraine Becker
persona.context: Has email but never checks it. Lives in iMessage. Asked the contractor 'just text me'.

preconditions: Customer phone has SMS-opt-in flag set (consent recorded at quote acceptance)

steps.action: Invoice is sent — both email and SMS dispatched
steps.expect: SMS body fits in one segment: 'Diego from Riley Roofing: $107.50 invoice ready — pay here: <short-link>. Reply STOP to opt out.'

steps.action: Taps the SMS link in Messages
steps.expect: Public-invoice page opens in mobile Safari; layout is mobile-first; amount is the first thing visible above the fold
Stripe charges; success state shown; SMS confirmation sent: 'Thanks! Receipt: <link>'
Outbound SMS dispatcher honors the opt-out; future reminders fall back to email only

evaluationCriteria.check: SMS-opened page doesn't hide the amount under hero chrome; one-thumb tap to pay
Customer never feels she had to 'sign up' for anything — pay and done
STOP keyword is honored across all SMS senders; consent state is persisted per phone number
SMS short-link uses HTTPS; landing page passes mobile a11y checks (tap targets ≥ 44px)
